summary: The test suite is thorough and well-structured, covering all four primary tagging conventions (latest, pr-{n}, v{semver}, branch-sha), all sanitization rules (lowercase, slash replacement, special-char removal, hyphen collapsing, trim), error handling for missing required inputs, a short-SHA edge case, combined-input scenarios, and deduplication. Organization is exemplary: a single BeforeAll dot-sources the implementation, and seven logically grouped Context blocks with clear 'should…' names make intent immediately obvious. Tests are stateless and independent, relying only on function return values — appropriate since the implementation is pure computation. Minor gaps that prevent a perfect rigor score: no test for whitespace-only BranchName/CommitSha (the implementation checks IsNullOrWhiteSpace but tests only check empty strings), no explicit test for PrNumber=0 being ignored, the helper ConvertTo-DockerTag is exercised only indirectly, and there is no test asserting that the master branch does not generate a branch-sha tag (parallel to the main-branch assertion). These are small omissions in an otherwise excellent suite that would reliably catch regressions across the full feature surface.
